races.name: Paris-Camembert
races.description: The 87th edition of the Norman classic runs 198km from Magnanville (Yvelines) to Livarot-Pays-d'Auge (Calvados) through the Pays d'Auge. The first 130km are flat and uneventful; the race explodes in the final 60km with 7 punchy climbs including the Mur des Champeaux and the Butte des Fondits, whose final passage comes just 9km from the finish line. Only 2 WT teams in the field (Decathlon CMA CGM, Groupama-FDJ United) — a classic opportunity for ProTeam and Continental riders. Defending champion: Lander Loockx (Unibet Rose Rockets, 2025).

broadcasts.notes: Broadcast on HBO Max France confirmed; exact start time of coverage not published. Race starts 11:05 CEST / 09:05 UTC.

data_confidence.notes: Start time 11:05 CEST / 09:05 UTC confirmed by DirectVelo (fictif 10:55). Distance 198km, route Magnanville–Livarot-Pays-d'Auge confirmed by official organiser GPSEO and PCS. 87th edition confirmed. 110 riders, 16 teams. Only WT teams: Decathlon CMA CGM and Groupama-FDJ United. Venturini won La Roue Tourangelle on Sunday. Broadcast: HBO Max France is cited by Le Mag du Cyclisme but no broadcast time confirmed — this is the only known broadcaster. No UK, US, or international broadcaster identified. Result not yet available at file generation time — race finishing approximately 15:30 CEST.

issues: Paris-Camembert was omitted from the initial March 31 file — apologies for the error. It is a 1.1 UCI Europe Tour race confirmed for today.
Result not yet available at generation time (race starts 09:05 UTC, expected finish ~13:30 UTC).
HBO Max France is the only identified broadcaster; no broadcast time confirmed. No international streaming options identified for this race.
G.P. Emilia (1.1, Italy) was cancelled for 2026 — confirmed by PCS. Not to be confused with the Giro dell'Emilia (October).
In Flanders Fields Men recap (March 29): Jasper Philipsen wins sprint after Van der Poel/Van Aert 2-up caught at 1km. Podium: 1. Philipsen 2. Tobias Lund Andresen 3. Christophe Laporte.
In Flanders Fields Women recap (March 29): Lorena Wiebes wins record 3rd consecutive edition in sprint of 5 after Kemmelberg. Podium: 1. Wiebes 2. Fleur Moors 3. Karlijn Swinkels.
